era: 1959-1966 core. Garage Rock. E=7 -- instrumental rock powerhouse. Tacoma. Golden Crest Records.
disambiguation: NOT Bob Marley's Wailers (Jamaica, 1963). Tacoma Wailers formed 1959 -- four years earlier. Different genre, different country, different history.

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST NATIONALLY CHARTING PNW ROCK BAND. 'Tall Cool One' (1959) reached #36 Billboard Hot 100. An independent label record from Tacoma competing in the national Top 40. The Wailers opened the door for all subsequent PNW bands.
theory_nodes.description: First PNW rock band to chart nationally.

theory_nodes.concept: Buck Ormsby dual role: Wailers bassist AND Etiquette Records founder. Simultaneously an artist and the infrastructure builder for the Northwest Sound. The scene's most important structural figure.
theory_nodes.description: Ormsby as musician and label executive.

theory_nodes.concept: Northwest Sound template: The Wailers invented the formula before The Sonics existed. Organ + guitar + driving rhythm + aggressive vocals = Northwest Sound. The Wailers built the template; the Sonics pushed it to E=8.

cross_references.relationship: Same city, same era, successor relationship. The Sonics pushed the Wailers' template to E=8 extremity.
Both PNW instrumental rock. The Ventures built global career; the Wailers built the local foundation that made the Ventures possible.
The Wailers reached #36 four years before the Kingsmen's 'Louie Louie'. Both essential to PNW garage legacy.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 vs E=2. The first nationally charting PNW rock band against the iconic PNW native trout. Both symbols of their respective worlds.

milestones: FIRST NATIONALLY CHARTING PNW ROCK BAND
